frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, I get that safety is important, but why do some girls just assume the worst about *all* men? Like, main toh achha insaan hoon, kabhi kisi ko bother nahi kiya, but still, this general suspicion feels so unfair to guys like us.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment dismisses women's wariness ('why assume the worst?') by centering the speaker's own perceived goodness ('main toh achha insaan hoon') and feeling unfairly generalized, thereby challenging the validity of women's collective safety concerns. It encodes the 'Not All Men' dismissal and feeling personally insulted by women discussing patterns of male behavior.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 23. Single. A college student in Bengaluru. You are in a WhatsApp group with seven male college friends. Someone has shared a meme that says women spend two hours putting on makeup and then get angry when men call them fake. You are reacting to the meme and adding your own comment about women who wear heavy makeup and what they are hiding and whether men have a right to know what a woman actually looks like before committing to a relationship.
